headings.text: Species Atlas of the Global Ocean

paragraphs.text:
The atlas aggregates IUCN Red List assessments, IPBES range models, and OBIS occurrence
      records into a single navigable map. Hotspot markers are scaled to the local
      Shannon biodiversity index H' and tinted along a teal→coral axis to
      indicate aggregate conservation status. Featured species in the side panel are
      drawn from the IUCN Top‑12 priority list for FY2024.
Peer-reviewed ocean health data, visualized for climate researchers, journalists, and educators worldwide.

Diet Fish, squid Threat Overfishing Lion's Mane Jelly Cyanea capillata Increasing Range Boreal Atlantic Diet Plankton, fish Threat Bloom expansion Vaquita Phocoena sinus Critical Range Gulf of California Diet Small fish, squid Threat Gillnet bycatch Primary Threats by Taxon 38% Overfishing 22% Bycatch 15% Warming 11% Plastic 8% Acidification 6% Habitat loss Taxonomic distribution across 8 marine phyla Concentric ring segments scaled to known described species, OBIS 2024 release 247k SPECIES Chordata · 28% Mollusca · 19% Arthropoda · 17% Cnidaria · 11% Echinodermata · 9% Porifera · 7% Annelida · 6% Other · 3% Chordata 28% Mollusca 19% Arthropoda 17% Cnidaria 11% Echinodermata 9% Porifera 7% Annelida 6% Other 3% Data sources: IUCN Red List v2024-3 OBIS WoRMS IPBES Global Assessment FishBase GBIF Marine Atlas alert · 41% of featured taxa show declining trend Of the 247k currently described marine species, 41% of the IUCN priority-12 sample exhibit a downward population trajectory over the last decade. Cetaceans and chondrichthyans remain disproportionately affected by industrial fishing pressure.
